key_insight: SevenNet-Polar extends equivariant graph neural networks to predict Born effective charges alongside energy, forces, and stress with high accuracy and strong transferability.
engineering_relevance: It enables faster charge-aware atomistic simulations under electric fields, making large-scale studies of defects, interfaces, and ionic materials more practical.
method: SevenNet-based equivariant multitask GNN for energy, forces, stress, and BEC.
Separate BEC-only predictors and all-in-one multitask models for ZrO2, Li3PO4, and perovskites.
FlashTP acceleration for large-scale inference on multi-GPU systems and consumer GPUs.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports BEC RMSE as low as 0.0043 e for BEC-only models and 0.0029 e for multitask models, with energy/force/stress errors of 1.0 meV/atom, 12 meV/angstrom, and 0.05 GPa, respectively.
watch_item: Scaling behavior for diagonal and off-diagonal BEC components is less favorable than for energy, forces, and stress, so accuracy may degrade in more challenging or out-of-distribution environments.
paper_title: SevenNet-Polar for MultiTask Prediction of Energy, Forces, Stress, and Born Effective Charges: Development and Application to ZrO$_2$, Li$_3$PO$_4$, and Perovskites
